source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.464 (scan idx 463), printed 'TABVLA XXI' at foot
family: carmen-cancellatum (dense grille: capitals I-V-R-E-M-E-R-V-I tile the whole field in alternating black/white ornamental blocks; interstitial verse)
engraver_note: Foot reads 'TABVLA XXI' but this is the engraver's MISPRINT — content/sequence place it as XXII (it follows the true XXI Costalta plate). Recorded as anomaly per zero-correction rule; numbering here = XXII by position.
title_diplomatic: Ob expugnatas Gentes Ethnicas, confutatos veteres, et futuros errores, reformatos mores Christiani Populi; nec non ob vitae puritatem, Doctrinae securitatem, rationumque pondus, et efficaciam. ANGELICI DOCTORIS Cognomentum IVRE MERVI.
title_translation: For having vanquished the heathen nations, refuted the ancients, reformed future errors and the morals of the Christian people; and for purity of life, security of doctrine, weight and efficacy of reasoning — the title of ANGELIC DOCTOR he has EARNED BY RIGHT.
dedication_diplomatic: Illustrissimo et Reverendissimo D.D. Iosepho Ciantes Marsicensium Episc. etc. Domino suo Colendissimo. D. Michael Weyms C.D.Q.
note: The grille is a PALINDROME (IVRE MERVI reads identically reversed), so the cancellatum reads the motto identically along forward and retrograde lattice paths — a built-in recurrens symmetry.
calibration_role: Palindromic-grille cancellatum: forward and retrograde readings of IVRE MERVI are identical, an exact recurrens fixpoint (echo). Tests op_is_echo at the motto level and confirms the grille's symmetry collapses |Σ| in a measurable way.
